questions: You're content where you are in life. Your manager asks where you see yourself in five years during your annual review. What do you say?
You're content where you are in life. A friend gets a big promotion and asks if you've thought about going for a leadership role too. How do you respond?
You're content where you are in life. You see a job listing for a senior position at a competitor that pays significantly more. What do you think?
You're content where you are in life. Your college roommate just launched a startup and invites you to come aboard as a co-founder. What's your reaction?
You're content where you are in life. A mentor tells you that you're underperforming relative to your potential and should aim higher. How do you take that?
You're content where you are in life. You finish a project at work that goes well and your team celebrates. What are you thinking about afterward?
You're content where you are in life. You're at a networking event and someone asks what your biggest professional goal is right now. What do you say?
You're content where you are in life. Your partner suggests you go back to school for an advanced degree that could open new doors. How do you feel about that?
You're content where you are in life. You get an email from a recruiter saying you'd be perfect for a director-level role at a growing company. What do you do?
You're content where you are in life. You're watching a documentary about someone who built an empire from nothing. What goes through your mind?
